title: Isolepis steudelii
intro: Isolepis steudelii, also known as the annual club-rush, is a species of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae) characterized by its compact, tufted growth habit and small, yellowish-green flowers. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Isolepis steudelii is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Isolepis steudelii is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into cough syrups and other respiratory remedies.
taxonomy: Isolepis steudelii is a species of sedge, belonging to the family Cyperaceae. This classification places it within the order Poales. At the family level, Isolepis steudelii is part of a large group of monocotyledonous plants. It is further classified into the genus Isolepis, which comprises various species of sedges. The classification of Isolepis steudelii reflects its close relationships with other members of the sedge family.
